title: Bot Payments (In-Chat Payment Processing via Payment Provider Abstraction)
description: Bots can initiate payment requests in-chat. Users pay via the platform's native payment UI, which delegates to external PSPs (Stripe, YooKassa, etc.) via a provider abstraction layer. The platform never handles raw card data — PCI scope is minimized to the PSP redirect flow. Funds flow: User → PSP → Merchant (bot operator).

pci_scope_minimization.strategy: SAQ-A level: platform renders an iframe/webview pointing to PSP-hosted payment page. Card data never touches platform servers.
pci_scope_minimization.card_data_flow: User browser/app → PSP iframe → PSP backend. Platform receives only payment token (opaque reference).
pci_scope_minimization.tokenization: PSP returns charge_id + payment_token. Platform stores payment_token for refund operations — never raw PAN/CVV.

participants.bot_operator: Business registering a bot with payment capability. Must complete KYC with chosen PSP.
participants.payment_provider: Configured per bot: Stripe / YooKassa / Razorpay (extensible). Registered in payment_providers table.
participants.platform: Orchestration layer: routes payment requests, receives callbacks, notifies bots.

schema.title: VARCHAR(255)
schema.payload: TEXT(128) (opaque bot-defined string for order tracking)

payment_invoices.rls: Bot can read its own invoices. User can read invoices where user_id = auth.uid(). No cross-access.

transitions: pending → pre_checkout: user taps 'Pay', passes pre-checkout validation (shipping, phone if required)
pre_checkout → paid: PSP webhook confirms successful charge
pre_checkout → failed: PSP webhook indicates decline
pending | pre_checkout → expired: expires_at reached without payment
paid → refunded: bot operator requests refund via API
payment_state_machine.idempotency: PSP webhooks may duplicate. UNIQUE constraint on provider_charge_id ensures exactly-once state transition.

pre_checkout_answer.timeout: Bot must respond within 10 seconds or platform auto-rejects

psp_webhook.security: HMAC-SHA256 signature validation per PSP spec. Unsigned webhooks rejected (HTTP 401).
psp_webhook.idempotency: webhook event ID deduplicated in Redis (TTL 48h)

security.price_tampering: Client sends payment; server validates amount against invoice record — client-side amount claim NEVER trusted.
security.double_charge: Idempotency key on invoice creation prevents duplicate charges. paid_at nullable, set exactly once via CAS.
security.bot_impersonation: Invoice creation requires signed bot API token (JWT, HS256 with bot secret in Vault).
security.refund_abuse: Refund only via authenticated bot API. Platform marks invoice as refunded and calls PSP refund API.
